ocr.text: The table presents detailed data on various PBDE congeners, including their IUPAC numbers, detection rates, and concentration
metrics. The first congener, 2,4,4-Tri-BDE, with IUPAC number 28, shows 295 detectable instances (97%), a minimum of ND,
a 25th percentile of 1, median of 2, 75th percentile of 4, and a maximum of 50. Next, the 2,2′,4,4′-Tetra-BDE (IUPAC 47) has
302 detectable instances (99%), with ND as the minimum, 16 at the 25th percentile, 28 as the median, 54 at the 75th percentile,
and a maximum of 1,430. The 2,3′,4,4′-Tetra-BDE (IUPAC 66) has 139 detectable instances (46%), with ND for both minimum
and 25th percentile, ND for median, 0.5 at the 75th percentile, and a maximum of 11. For the 2,2′,3,4,4′-Penta-BDE (IUPAC
85), 196 instances (65%) were detected, with ND minimum, ND 25th percentile, 0.5 median, 1 at the 75th percentile, and a
maximum of 27.
PBDE congener  IUPAC number  Detectable [no. (%)]  Minimum  25th percentile  Median  75th percentile  Maximum
The 2,2′,4,4′,5-Penta-BDE (IUPAC 99) shows 278 detectable (92%), minimum ND, 3 at 25th percentile, 5 median, 11 at 75th
percentile, and a maximum of 299. The 2,2′,4,4′,6-Penta-BDE (IUPAC 100) has 300 detectable (99%), with ND minimum, 2 at
the 25th percentile, 5 median, 10 at 75th percentile, and a maximum of 188. The 2,2′,4,4′,5,5′-Hexa-BDE (IUPAC 153) has 302
detectable (99%), with ND minimum, 3 at 25th percentile, 6 median, 14 at 75th percentile, and a maximum of 229. The
2,3,4,4′,5,6-Hexa-BDE (IUPAC 154) shows 163 detectable (54%), with ND minimum, ND 25th percentile, 0.3 median, 0.7 at
75th percentile, and a maximum of 13. The 2,2′,3,4,4′,5′,6-Hepta-BDE (IUPAC 183) indicates 35 detectable (12%), with ND
minimum and percentiles, and a maximum of 15. Finally, the total PBDEs (∑PBDEs) reports 303 detectable, with a minimum
of 1.2, 25th percentile of 28, median of 51, 75th percentile of 102, and a maximum of 2,010.
